question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the compareAndSet in getStartAfterFilename() where lastStreamFile is set from the database's last stream file, by corrupting the database record of the last processed file, causing the importer to re-download and re-process already-imported files and export duplicate records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback to StreamFilename.EPOCH when lastStreamFile is empty, causing the importer to attempt to download all stream files from the beginning of time, overwhelming the system and causing recent records to be delayed or missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart() in shouldParse(), by injecting a stream file with a consensusStart equal to the previous file's consensusEnd, causing the current file to be silently skipped with a warning and its records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: shouldParse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hash chain check in shouldParse() where a HashMismatchException is thrown when previousHash does not match, by corrupting the hash of the last stored stream file in the database, causing all subsequent stream files to be rejected and their records to be permanently missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an exception during doParse() for a specific stream file, causing parse() to log an error and rethrow, which if uncaught by the caller leaves the last AtomicReference unchanged and causes the next parse() call to re-check the same previous file, potentially skipping records or causing duplicate processing on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: parse(List)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a batch of stream files where one file in the middle throws an exception during doParse(), causing parse(List) to abort without calling doFlush() for any file in the batch, resulting in all records from that batch being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getLast() fallback to streamFileRepository.findLatest() when the in-memory last reference is null (e.g., after a restart), by manipulating the database to return a stale or incorrect latest stream file, causing shouldParse() to skip valid new stream files and export missing records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: doFlush()] Can an unprivileged external user cause streamFileListener.onEnd() to throw an exception during doFlush(), preventing last.set(streamFile) from being called, so that the next parse() call re-processes the same stream file and exports duplicate records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.parser.exclude with a broad transaction filter via an externalized config, causing DateRangeCalculator.getFilter() to exclude entire categories of valid transactions from being parsed, resulting in those transaction records being permanently missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: newDateRangeFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.startDate to a value after the last processed file's consensusStart via an externalized config, causing newDateRangeFilter() to set filterStartDate to max(startDate, lastFileInstant) which skips all records between lastFileInstant and startDate, creating a gap of missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLastStreamFile()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.startDate to a value before Instant.EPOCH via an externalized config, causing getLastStreamFile() to compute an effectiveStartDate of Instant.EPOCH and create a dummy stream file with index null, breaking the block index sequence exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getLastStreamFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the blockProperties.isEnabled() check in getLastStreamFile(), by enabling block stream mode via an externalized config, causing effectiveStartDate to be set to Instant.EPOCH regardless of the database state, causing the importer to re-download all block stream files from the beginning and export duplicate records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: filter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DateRangeFilter.filter() boundary condition where timestamp == start is included but a record with timestamp exactly at the boundary between two filter windows is processed twice (once by each filter), causing duplicate records to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: notify()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the BatchStreamFileNotifier's ArrayBlockingQueue to fill up by flooding the system with stream files faster than the parser can consume them, causing notify() to block indefinitely via queue.put(), halting the downloader thread and causing all subsequent records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: handle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flush() logic in BatchStreamFileNotifier where a stream file with getCount() returning a very large value causes items.addAndGet() to overflow Long.MAX_VALUE, triggering an immediate flush with corrupted item counts and causing incorrect batch boundaries that lead to records being processed out of order on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: flush()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lag calculation in flush() where DomainUtils.now() - streamFile.getConsensusEnd() is computed, by supplying a stream file with a consensusEnd far in the future, causing lag to be negative and always triggering an immediate flush, breaking the batching logic and causing records to be exported individually with incorrect batch metadata to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: verified()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a stream file with an unsupported StreamType (e.g., BLOCK) to reach the verified() method in BatchStreamFileNotifier, triggering an IllegalArgumentException that propagates up and halts the notifier thread, causing all subsequent records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: reconcile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ACCUMULATE remediation strategy in BalanceReconciliationService.reconcile() by causing a balance discrepancy in an early balance file, which then propagates forward through all subsequent reconciliation windows, masking ongoing incorrect records being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FIFTY_BILLION_HBARS check in getAccountBalances() by causing a balance file to be imported with a total that does not equal 50 billion HBARs (e.g., due to a missing account record), causing a ReconciliationException that halts reconciliation and masks the underlying missing record on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: reconcileCryptoTransfers()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the errata filter in CRYPTO_TRANSFER_QUERY (errata is null or errata <> 'DELETE') by causing a legitimate transfer to be incorrectly marked with errata='DELETE' in the database, causing it to be excluded from reconciliation and masking a missing crypto transfer record on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.network to a value like '-attacker' via an externalized config, causing getNetwork() to return an empty string and getNetworkPrefix() to return 'attacker', leading to HederaNetwork.getBucketName() returning an empty string and the importer failing to resolve the correct cloud bucket, resulting in all records being missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file with a filename shorter than 10 characters, causing getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException during the substring(0, 10) call, halting the archive operation and causing the stream file's records to be lost before they are exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorContinue() handler in downloadAndParseSigFiles() which silently swallows errors from individual nodes, by causing all honest nodes to return errors while a malicious node succeeds, resulting in only the malicious node's signatures being collected and its incorrect stream file reaching consensus and being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout() operator in downloadAndParseSigFiles() by causing honest nodes to respond slowly (e.g., via network congestion), causing their signatures to be dropped by the timeout, while a fast malicious node's signatures are the only ones collected, allowing incorrect records to reach consensus and be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.timeout to a very short duration (e.g., 1 second) via an externalized config, causing most node signature downloads to time out and only a small subset of signatures to be collected, making it easier for a minority of nodes to reach the 1/3 consensus threshold with incorrect records?
